blocks.content: The game of Truth or Dare had begun, and the first question was over.
Even that was enough to create a subtle tension among the students.
Though it was an unspoken rule in the senior class never to bring up what was said during the game, the participants couldn't help but feel affected.
Everyone was fiercely engaged in a battle of wits.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: So this is how it is. If I hadn't known, it could have been a disaster.
Shirone felt a bit more at ease and looked at Amy.
No matter what question came up, he just had to point to her, avoiding early psychological warfare.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: She had gone through this process last year in the senior class. For Shirone, a new senior, her help was more valuable than gold.
Amy met his gaze and shrugged.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Thank you, Amy. Really, thank you.
The psychological pressure disguised as tradition.
Being able to avoid early psychological attacks from competitors was definitely an advantage in the senior class.

blocks.speaker: Pony
blocks.content: Haha, alright! Let's move on. Pony, why don't you go next? Everyone has to do it at least once.
Pony, maintaining her icy expression, asked Neide, who stood opposite her.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Who was the first person you saw when you got here?
It was a simple question, leaving no room for psychological games. She seemed uninterested in Truth or Dare.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Really? That's it? Can't you make it more interesting?

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: Forget it. This is childish.
Neide looked at the Class Two students. He could find meaning in any question if he wanted, but since Pony had asked lightly, he answered honestly.

blocks.speaker: Neide
blocks.content: The first person I saw was that guy.
All eyes turned to the person Neide pointed at.
A tall, lanky man with perfect curls and tanned skin from long sun exposure. His prominent cheekbones made him look even older.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: That's Pisho.
Pisho was the oldest in the senior class at twenty-five, though he looked thirty-five.
His specialty was insect magic, dealing with insects, a unique field among various magics.

Insect magic was more academically developed than magical. Most specialists in this field became scholars, but Pisho stayed in school until twenty-five to get a mage's license for better opportunities.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Alright, I'll ask the next question.
Screamer's opponent was Dante. As the top students of Class Two and Class Three, it felt like a showdown between leaders.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Who do you think you could easily beat? Someone so weak you could crush them with a finger.
Even though the question wasn't directed at him, Shirone felt tense.
No matter who Dante chose, it would hurt someone's pride. Any choice would leave lingering resentment.
Dante frowned, deep in thought.
The Class Two students watched him with sharp eyes, daring him to choose them.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Hmm, this is really tough.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Just be honest. It's an unspoken rule that there will be no retaliation for any choice.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Can't I just say all of Class Two? They all seem about the same.
The explosive atmosphere deflated instantly. Class Two's expressions turned cold, and Shirone's eyes widened.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Only someone like Dante, once the kingdom's top prospect, could say such a thing.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Sorry, but that's not allowed. You have to pick just one. That's the rule of Truth or Dare.

blocks.speaker: Dante
blocks.content: Alright, then I'll pick you. Since you're the top of Class Two, choosing you means everyone else is below me.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This guy is really crazy.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: This guy is really crazy.
Dante Airhein. The top prospect even at the Royal Magic School.
Even now, he was considered one of the top candidates for graduation.
But that was based on advanced class standards.
Once past the Iron Gate, advanced class rankings no longer mattered.
No matter how skilled, one mistake could lead to a downfall in the competitive senior class.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This is why I hate rookies. They get pampered in the advanced class and think they're invincible.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Can I ask the next question?
Maya raised her hand.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Go ahead.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Who's so adorable that you want to hug and kiss them right now?
Shirone swallowed nervously. What would she ask?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I just have to choose Amy. We've already agreed on this. No matter the question, it's no big deal.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Uh... what?
Even Amy seemed caught off guard by this unexpected question.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: This wasn't about choosing an answer. She had asked the question knowing the answer.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What should I do? Should I pick someone else?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He thought it over but couldn't find a better solution than sticking to the plan.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I... I choose...
Blushing, Shirone pointed at Amy.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Wow! Really? You two are actually dating?
Eider exaggeratedly reacted, putting pressure on Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Screamer
blocks.content: Wow, you had the courage to choose honestly. Since it's out in the open, why not show us?
Ignoring him, Shirone looked back at Maya, who was laughing as if she found it all amusing.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Why had Maya asked him such a question?

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He might never get the chance to ask, but this question couldn't pressure a newcomer, nor did it seem like something she would care about.

blocks.speaker: Luman
blocks.content: Alright, who in Class Two do you think is definitely not a virgin?
Pony's golden eyebrows furrowed angrily.

blocks.speaker: Pony
blocks.content: Disgusting...
This was why she disliked the senior class traditions. Why should a royal like her be subject to such questions?

blocks.speaker: Luman
blocks.content: I mean, who looks like they'd sleep with any guy?
The female students seemed to burn with indignation.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Hmm, I see.
Iruki rested his chin on his hand, trying to find a basis for his answer.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Of course, he couldn't tell just by looking. But he wasn't one to rely on intuition.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Excluding Amy, he considered the four Class Two girls: Pony, Maya, Suabi, and Dorothy.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: First, he looked at Pony.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: She turned her head slightly, as if insulted.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: A royal...

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Pony's first impression was of a cold, noble ice flower.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: As a royal, she likely didn't mingle with common nobles. Statistically, her pool of male acquaintances would be smaller.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Okay, not her. Next, Maya?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Maya watched with interest, curious about his answer. Though she was a candidate, she seemed to enjoy the situation.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: She's too openly happy. That's a bit...

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Iruki turned to Suabi.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: With her short, braided brown hair, she hadn't spoken a word since the event began and avoided eye contact.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Her gentle, rabbit-like appearance matched her timid demeanor, and she seemed genuinely terrified.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: She's too shy. Isn't she a utility specialist?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Utility magic supported other mages rather than acting alone.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: While sound magic excelled in wide-area buffs, utility magic focused on individual enhancement with excellent effects.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: In the professional world, utility specialists were highly sought after, but fewer mages chose it due to its dependency on others.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Given her timid nature, Suabi's choice of utility magic made sense.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: So that doll moves with an algorithm. I thought it was like a harvester.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Iruki made his decision.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: I choose you.
Despite the attention, Dorothy didn't react. It was as if she lived in another world.
Iruki awkwardly lowered his hand.

blocks.speaker: Dorothy
blocks.content: Why me?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: I observed resignation and resistance. If someone were truly promiscuous, they wouldn't want to reveal it. Based on that, you seemed the most emotionally detached.

blocks.speaker: Dorothy
blocks.content: You're right.
The boys looked at Dorothy in shock.
The boys looked back at Dorothy with surprised eyes.

blocks.speaker: Dorothy
blocks.content: I love all men.

blocks.speaker: Dorothy
blocks.content: Iruki. I love you too.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: And I love women too.

blocks.speaker: Dorothy
blocks.content: Huh?
An exclamation of surprise came from someone.
Regardless, Dorothy hugged the tin doll and slowly lowered her gaze.
The gazes that had been focused on Dorothy scattered in different directions. It was an instinctive reaction.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: What is she talking about? She's out of her mind.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Shirone was equally bewildered. Competing with people like this in the future seemed daunting.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Especially since Class One hadn't even stepped up yet.
